channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge to Lead Australia's Team at Commonwealth Games
item.description: Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge have been named as Australia's flag bearer and inaugural baton bearer for the Commonwealth Games, marking their third appearance in the event. Kennedy, a pole vault champion...

item.title: Google Messages Themes Help Prevent Accidental Miscommunication
item.description: The article discusses how users can leverage Google Messages themes to avoid sending messages to the wrong recipient. With multiple chats active, it's easy to accidentally reply to the incorrect threa...

item.title: Yemen's Houthis escalate regional tensions by targeting critical trade routes
item.description: The article discusses the escalating conflict involving Yemen's Houthi rebels, who are increasingly aligning with Iran's interests. This alignment poses a significant risk to global trade by targeting...

item.title: Andy Burnham to Review Early Prisoner Release Policy Amid Public Outcry Over Killer Releases
item.description: Andy Burnham, the Labour leader, has announced plans to scrutinize an early prisoner release policy set to commence in autumn 2026, following widespread public backlash over the potential release of t...

item.title: Elburgon Senior School Closes After Dormitory Fire Destroys Student Belongings
item.description: A fire broke out at Elburgon Senior School in Molo, Nakuru, on July 21, 2026, destroying a dormitory that housed 147 Form Three students. Two fire engines from TimSales and Nakuru County Government re...

item.title: DILG Confirms Security Measures in Place for 2026 SONA
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has confirmed that all security protocols are fully operational ahead of President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s State of the Nation Addr...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi criticizes police crackdown during student protests, calls for Modi's apology
item.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has condemned the police actions against student protesters during the Cockroach Janta Party demonstration in Delhi, demanding Prime Minister Narendra Modi apologize for t...

item.title: Ongoing Investigation into Potential Unmarked Graves at Kamloops Residential School Site Faces Funding Challenges

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Hearings Continue into Corruption Cases
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry into corruption within the criminal justice system is ongoing, with hearings continuing on Wednesday, July 22, 2026. The commission, established to investigate syste...

item.title: ICC member states to vote on prosecutor Khan's removal amid misconduct allegations
item.description: Member states of the International Criminal Court (ICC) are set to vote on whether to remove chief prosecutor Karim Khan over allegations of sexual misconduct. The vote, scheduled for Friday, comes am...

item.title: Nigerian woman advocates for vitiligo awareness after relationship struggles
item.description: A Nigerian woman named Blessing Opeyemi Oduola shared her story about how her boyfriend ended their relationship after she was diagnosed with vitiligo. She revealed that he gradually distanced himself...

item.title: PM Mwigulu Launches NBC Dodoma Marathon with High Participation
item.description: The Prime Minister of Tanzania, Mwigulu, is set to officially start the NBC Dodoma Marathon, which has attracted a significant number of participants. The event, which is part of the country's efforts...

item.title: Blood vs Duty: Marcus Dedicates Time to Promote Jalil's Recognition | Episode 78
item.description: The latest episode of 'Blood vs Duty' highlights Marcus Reyes' efforts to elevate the profile of Jalil Abubakar, a key character in the series. The episode, titled 'Episode 78,' focuses on Marcus' com...

item.title: Nigeria's Anthem Change Sparks Debate on National Identity
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Nigeria's decision to revert to the old national anthem 'Arise, O Compatriots' after its replacement in 2024. It highlights how millions of Nigerians ...

item.title: Tracey Cox's Insights on Modern Relationship Advice and Generational Differences
item.description: The article explores the evolving dynamics of marriage in modern times, contrasting the experiences of midlife women with their mothers' generation. Tracey Cox, a UK sex and relationships expert, crit...

item.title: GOP Concerned Over Trump's Potential Impact on Midterm Elections
item.description: Republican campaign operatives are deeply worried that President Donald Trump's aggressive military actions in Iran could jeopardize the GOP's congressional majorities and 'ruin his second term,' acco...

item.title: Gambling Addiction and Exploitation: A Couple's Tragic Fate
item.description: The article details the harrowing case of Ray and Marlene Kasurinen, a couple allegedly groomed and exploited by the Hellenic Club through predatory gambling practices. It highlights how the club’s ta...

item.title: Mentally Ill Man's Corpse Found Dead in Ondo Sparks Health Concerns
item.description: Residents of Ore, Ondo State, are worried about environmental and public health risks after discovering the corpse of a mentally ill man along the Ore overhead bridge. The body, wrapped in a yellow cl...

item.title: Concept Academy delivers AI Masterclass in Rwanda as Tanzania boosts financial initiatives
item.description: The article highlights key developments in Tanzania and beyond. Concept Academy celebrated a significant achievement by delivering an AI Masterclass in Rwanda, marking progress in technology education...

item.title: West Bengal Assembly suspends TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh over factional conflict
item.description: Trinamool Congress MLA Kunal Ghosh was suspended from the West Bengal Assembly for the remainder of the day after disrupting proceedings by interrupting rebel faction leader Akhruzzaman during a debat...
